prologue.title: A Normal Morning
prologue.narrator: The ends of the world rarely begin with a great noise. Sometimes they start with a cup of coffee.
prologue.text:
You wake up with the pale morning light on the wall.

The city outside is already living its ordinary little life.

Your phone vibrates.

Message from Nora:

> Coffee in 20 minutes?
> I'll pay if you promise not to tell me about your dead plants yet.

Nora.

Your friend's long enough to know your silences.
Too close to lie properly.
Too stubborn to admit when she's scared.

On the radio, one voice speaks of international tensions.

You're only half listening.

Like everybody else.

choices.text: Reply to Nora with a joke

radio_matin.title: The Morning Radio
radio_matin.narrator: Bad news often looks less serious when read quietly.
radio_matin.text:
You're raising the sound.

> Authorities call for calm.
> No immediate risk is reported.
> The civil defence exercises planned today are maintained.

Then an ad for cereal cuts the show.

Your phone's still vibrating.

Nora:

> Did you go back to sleep?
> If yes, I keep your croissant.

You take your keys.

cafe_blague.title: The Café des Petites Habitudes
cafe_blague.narrator: It is often tiny habits that make the world feel strong.
cafe_blague.text:
On your way, you send a message to Nora.

> My plants are not dead.
> They're pretending to get attention.

She answers in three seconds:

> Like you.

When you get to the coffee shop, she's already here.

Hair tied any way.
Military jacket too big.
Smile at someone who decided to mock the world before the world laughs at it.

She's raising your croissant.

**"I almost ate it. I was heroic. *

For a few minutes, everything is simple.

Hot coffee.
Fine rain.
Nora grinning against the spoon too small.

On television, political leaders shake jaws.

Nobody's really looking.

choices.text: Propose to go through the covered market
Ask Nora if she looks tired

cafe_simple.title: The Café, this Morning
cafe_simple.narrator: It is often tiny habits that make the world feel strong.
cafe_simple.text:
When you get to the cafe, Nora's already here.

She ordered for you.

**"I took the same as usual. If you've changed your taste during the night, I'm sorry for you. *

Rain draws lines on the glass.

On TV, a red banner slides down the screen.

> MAJOR TENSIONS — AUTHORITIES APPLY TO CALME

Nora follows your gaze.

For a second, her smile cracks.

Then she lifts her shoulders.

**"They always call calm when they don't know what to say. *

choices.text: Offer a small walk before coming home

nora_fatigue.title: The Cernes of Nora
nora_fatigue.narrator: Some people hide secrets better than fatigue.
nora_fatigue.text:
You notice her rings.

Not just a bad night.

Several.

Nora turns her cup into her hands.

**"I worked late. *

You know she works at a research center near the old military hospital.

On his wrist, a small bandage goes beyond his sleeve.

When she sees your look, she folds the fabric.

Too fast.

**"Don't look that way. It's nothing. Occupational medical tests. *

Then she smiled.

Not her real smile.

**"Want to walk a little? I need some air. *

choices.text: Ask her how her family is doing

indice_pansement.title: The Dressing
indice_pansement.narrator: Important clues know how to disguise themselves in ordinary detail.
indice_pansement.text:
You ask him if everything's okay.

Nora looks down on her wrist.

**"They asked me to reanalyze. Nothing dramatic. *

She's trying to laugh.

**"I'm apparently compatible with too many machines. *

The sentence is strange.

She seems to regret saying it.

Before you can ask what she means, the coffee lights flash.

Just a second.

Then everything comes back.

choices.text: Take out the air with Nora

tv_cafe.title: La Télévision Muette
tv_cafe.narrator: Screens sometimes announce the end of the world before the world agrees to hear it.
tv_cafe.text:
You're watching TV.

The sound is cut, but the images are enough.

Cards.
Arrows.
Faces closed.
A presenter touching his earpiece like someone just told him not to panic.

Nora's watching too.

She whispers:

**"Not today..."**

You turn to her.

She pretends she didn't talk.

At the same time, all persons at the café are notified.

A brief sound.

Same.

journee_normale.title: The City Before
journee_normale.narrator: Before the panic, there is always a last moment when everything could have remained normal.
journee_normale.text:
You walk in the fine rain.

Nora talks about useless things.

The new coffee owner.
A dog that barks at 7:12.
Because distributors are now selling triangular sandwiches that are far too confident.

You're laughing.

So does she.

Then she stops in front of a bookstore.

In the window, a children's book shows a rocket.

Nora put her hand on the glass.

**"When I was little, I thought the antiatomic shelters were rockets upside down. We were going underground to go somewhere else. *

She takes her hand off.

**"That's stupid. *

But his voice isn't stupid.

choices.text: Ask her why she thinks about shelters

librairie.title: Bookshop
librairie.narrator: Some places seem built to survive disasters, even when they cannot.
librairie.text:
The bookstore smells of paper, dust and heating too old.

An old man ranks books by size.

Nora takes a collection of poetry.

**"If the world ends, I hope it will be polite to let me finish this page. *

The bookseller smiles.

**"The world has never been very polite. *

Radio from the skewer shop.

The music stops.

A serious beep sounds.

nora_sait.title: What Nora doesn't say
nora_sait.narrator: The silence of someone you know can make more noise than a mermaid.
nora_sait.text:
You ask what she knows.

Nora opens her mouth.

Close it.

Her phone is vibrating.

She reads.

His face changes.

No surprise.

Fear.

But no surprise.

**"We have to go. Now. *

At this moment, sirens begin.

They don't look like tests.

They're longer.

Lower.

Older.

alerte.title: Alert
alerte.narrator: Some sounds divide a life into two: before, and after.
alerte.text:
All phones scream at the same time.

> NATIONAL EMERGENCY
> PROBABLE STRATEGIC IMPACTS
> IMMEDIATELY JOIN AN ABRI
> ESTIMATED TIME: 18 MINUTES

The coffee becomes silent.

Then someone laughs.

A short, unbelieving laugh.

Then a cup falls.

Then the city explodes in motion.

Nora grabs your arm.

**"We don't have 18 minutes. They always lie wide to avoid riots. *

explication_interrompue.title: Not Now
explication_interrompue.narrator: There are questions the world refuses to wait for.
explication_interrompue.text:
You're holding Nora.

**"What do you know? *

She's holding her teeth.

**"Not now. *

**"Nora. *

She's looking at you.

**"If I explain, you will want to do what they want from you. And I can't let you do that. *

Before you can understand, a distant explosion shakes the windows.

Nora shoots you through the sleeve.

choices.text: Refuse to move without an answer

aider_cafe.title: First Seconds
aider_cafe.narrator: Humanity sometimes starts by picking up someone who has fallen.
aider_cafe.text:
A waitress cut herself with a broken cup.

A man stays frozen in front of the television.

You help the waitress get up.

Nora wants to leave.

You see his anger.

Then his face changes.

She grabs towels.

She knows how.

Too good.

The waitress whispers thanks.

Nora only looks at time on her phone.

14 minutes.

appel.title: The Appeal
appel.narrator: In disasters, phones become too fragile bridges.
appel.text:
You call.

The line's ringing.

Once.

Twice.

Nora shoots you through the sleeve.

**"We have to go. *

The line's picking up.

A familiar voice says your name.

But behind her, you hear the sirens.

Same sound.

You don't have enough words to say everything.

choices.text: Trying to explain what to do

rue_panique.title: The City of Court
rue_panique.narrator: When everyone flees at the same time, the city becomes an injured organism.
rue_panique.text:
The streets are full.

Cars blocked.
Continuous horns.
People running with bags too heavy.
Parents with children in pajamas.

An advertising screen continues to sell holidays at the sea.

Nora knows a passage through the old service galleries under the hospital.

**"There is a military shelter under the complex. If they opened it, we can come in. *

A child is crying near a spilled car.

Later, a soldier yells that the bridge is closed.

enfant_voiture.title: The Reversed Car
enfant_voiture.narrator: Saving someone sometimes takes exactly the time we don't have.
enfant_voiture.text:
The child is stuck in the back.

His mother's trying to open the door.

Nora swears, then help you shoot.

The door is strong.

A plane passes.

This time, lower.

You're shooting again.

The door finally gives way.

The mother holds her child against her.

But you wasted time.

Too much.

On the street, people running to the galleries have already disappeared.

Nora looks away.

Then she looks at you like you just hurt her.

**"That's why I can't let you choose. *

His voice trembles.

**"Because you will always choose others before you. *

A new tremor crosses the buildings.

This time, the windows explode at the end of the avenue.

choices.text: Tell Nora the child had to live

soldat.title: The Dam
soldat.narrator: The order sometimes stands only because no one has yelled loud enough yet.
soldat.text:
The soldier is young.

Too young.

He repeats:

**"The bridge is closed. Join the designated shelters. *

Nora keeps her head low.

The soldier is looking at you.

Then he looks at Nora.

His expression changes.

Recognition.

No Nora.

You.

He's carrying his hand on his radio.

Nora sees it.

She pushes you into the crowd.

**"Now. *

nora_fissure.title: The Fissure
nora_fissure.narrator: Under panic, some secrets begin to breathe.
nora_fissure.text:
You ask what she means.

Nora shakes her fists.

**"You always help. Even when it destroys you. *

**"Is this supposed to be a defect? *

She laughs without joy.

**"Today, yes. *

Military announcement stings:

> All citizens must join shelters.
> Medical priority to compatible subjects.

Nora's turning white.

Subject compatible.

Two words that shouldn't have counted.

But they matter.

arrestation.title: The Bad Look
arrestation.narrator: Being important is rarely good news when soldiers run to you.
arrestation.text:
You stay one second too much.

The soldier speaks on his radio.

**"I think I found it. *

Nora grabs your arm, but two soldiers are already turning around.

They don't shout your name.

They say a code.

**"S-12 subject visible. *

Nora hits the soldier with a shocking violence.

Not a heroine.

Someone who refuses to lose.

She's shooting you in the crowd.

galeries.title: The Service Galleries
galeries.narrator: Under the cities, secrets often have tired neon.
galeries.text:
Nora opens a metal door behind the old hospital.

You don't ask her how she knows the code.

She's typing him too fast.

The galleries smell dust, disinfectant and hot electricity.

On the wall, arrows indicate:

> ABRI B-7
> LABORATORY HEMATOLOGY
> RESERVED ACCESS

You stop at the word haematology.

Nora pushes you slowly.

**"Not that way. *

But his voice says the opposite.

choices.text: Ask why she knows the code

question_code.title: The Code
question_code.narrator: The most fragile lies are the ones we give too fast.
question_code.text:
**"Why do you know the code? *

Nora keeps walking.

**"I worked here. *

**"You told me near here. Not here. *

She's not answering right now.

**"There are a lot of things I told you wrong. *

**"And what does it matter? *

It stops.

The sirens vibrate into the walls.

**"That you remain alive. *

laboratoire_indice.title: The Forbidden Corridor
laboratoire_indice.narrator: A word on a door can hold a lifetime.
laboratoire_indice.text:
Files were dropped on a wagon.

Nora says your name.

Not strong.

But with great fear.

You get the first file.

Your name's on it.

Or rather:

> Subject S-12
> Exceptional serum response
> Post-radiological compatibility: 98.7%

Your blood.

The world is waning.

Nora pulls the file out of your hands.

**"Not like that. You didn't have to learn it like that. *

A distant explosion cuts the lights.

They come back in red.

choices.text: Put it back to later and run

premiere_verite.title: What You Are
premiere_verite.narrator: Some truths do not save anyone when they arrive too late.
premiere_verite.text:
Nora closes her eyes.

*"Your blood resists. *

She's talking fast.

**"Particles. Radiation. To what comes next. They found out after last winter's accident, when you gave your blood. They put me on the project because I knew you. *

**"With enough samples, they can make a serum. Not for everyone. Not immediately. But enough to rebuild something. *

You feel the cold coming before the rest.

**"What about sampling? *

Nora's looking at you.

**"They would kill you. *

choices.text: Run to the shelter to understand
Ask why she lied to you

nora_aveu_partiel.title: The Lie
nora_aveu_partiel.narrator: A lie can be a prison built with love.
nora_aveu_partiel.text:
**"Why didn't you tell me? *

Nora's shaking.

**"Because I knew what you would do. *

**"You would have accepted. Maybe not yet. But you would've accepted. Because there would have been children, sick people, people crying on television. And you... you would have said yes. *

She wipes her eyes with anger.

**"So I stole the data. I delayed protocol. I sabotaged their access. *

Then, below:

**"I may have condemned thousands of people before the bombs. But you were still breathing. *

abri_entree.title: The B-7 Shelter
abri_entree.narrator: Survival sometimes has the shape of a door that doesn't open for everyone.
abri_entree.text:
The door to shelter B-7 is huge.

Dozens of people are waiting in front.

A system repeats:

> LIMITED CAPACITY
> PRIORITY FOR ESSENTIAL PERSONS
> PRIORITY FOR MEDICAL PROFILES

The crowd is screaming.

A military doctor is trying to keep order.

When he sees you, his face freezes.

He whispers:

**"That's him. *

Nora stands in front of you.

**"No. *

A rumbling crosses the city above.

The first impact took place.

Not here.

Not yet.

medecin_revele.title: Protocol S-12
medecin_revele.narrator: People who want to save the world rarely talk like monsters.
medecin_revele.text:
The doctor raises his hands.

He doesn't look cruel.

Only exhausted.

**"Your blood contains an immune response that we cannot reproduce. Serum can save people exposed to fallout. Not all of them. But enough. *

Nora screams:

**"Don't listen to him. *

The doctor continues.

**"Full extraction is necessary. She'll be lethal. *

The crowd hears enough.

Faces turn to you.

No hatred.

Worse.

Hope.

Nora moves back to a side door.

**"Come with me. Now. *

combien.title: How many lives?
combien.narrator: The numbers are sometimes more unbearable than the screams.
combien.text:
You ask how much.

The doctor doesn't look away.

**"In this shelter? Three hundred and twelve. In the other sectors if we transmit the formula on time: several thousand. Maybe more. *

Nora whispers:

**"He may say. *

The doctor responds gently:

**"Yes. Maybe. That's all that's left in the world. *

Nora's looking at you.

**"And I certainly tell you. You're probably dying. *

The ceiling is shaking.

Dust falls like grey snow.

triage.title: The Triage
triage.narrator: Helping everyone is sometimes a way not to choose yet.
triage.text:
You help get the civilians in.

An old woman.
Two kids.
A wounded man.
The coffee waitress, still pale.

Nora helps you despite her.

She knows how to arrange an evacuation.

Too good.

The doctor is looking at you from the inside door.

He doesn't rush you.

It's worse.

He's waiting for you to understand.

Nora puts a hand on your shoulder.

**"We've helped enough. Now I'm saving you. *

nora_fuite.title: The Lateral Gate
nora_fuite.narrator: When someone loves you too much, it may look like a leak.
nora_fuite.text:
Nora shoots you through a side door.

Behind you, the voices rise.

The doctor says your name.

The door's closing.

Nora locks manually.

**"There is an old maintenance shuttle. It leads to the south tunnels. If we leave now, we can get out before the secondary breath. *

**"What about the others? *

She closes her eyes.

**"The others are not you. *

The sentence falls between you.

Monstrous.

Really.

I can't forget.

choices.text: Tell him you can't do that.

confrontation.title: What the World needs
confrontation.narrator: There is no sentence clean enough to measure one life against all others.
confrontation.text:
**"I can't let these people die. *

Nora looks at you like you just confirmed an old fear.

**"I know. *

She laughs without joy.

**"That's exactly why I lied. *

**"You had no right. *

**"No. *

She's not defending herself.

**"But I did it anyway. *

An automatic voice announces:

> ESTIMATED MAIN IMPACT: 04 MINUTES
> ABRI B-7 — FINAL SELLING IMMINENT

Nora poses her forehead against yours.

**"I do not want to be forgiven. I want you to live. *

choices.text: Go back to the extraction room

salle_extraction.title: The White Room
salle_extraction.narrator: Saving the world can be too clean a room with a chair in the middle.
salle_extraction.text:
The extraction room is white.

Too white.

A medical chair is waiting for you.

Cables.
Sterile pockets.
Machines already ready.

The doctor speaks softly.

**"We can launch the protocol. You'll lose consciousness quickly. You won't suffer long. *

Nora's coming behind you.

His face is ravaged.

**"No. *

The doctor's not looking at her.

**"He must choose. *

The automatic voice announces:

> MAIN ESTIMATED IMPACT: 03 MINUTES

Everything is reduced to you.

To Nora.

In the chair.

To the lives we don't see.

tunnel_final.title: The South Tunnels
tunnel_final.narrator: Running away isn't always cowardice. Sometimes it's just choosing who you refuse to lose.
tunnel_final.text:
You run into the south tunnels.

The city trembles above.

Nora knows every branch line.

She prepared this.

You understand it now.

Not since this morning.

For weeks.

She planned a way out of the lab.

A way to keep you alive.

Even if the world needed you.

At the end of the tunnel, a door leads outwards.

Behind you, the speakers repeat:

> PROTOCOL S-12 UNLANCED
> LOSSES ESTIMATED: CRITICAL

Nora doesn't turn around.

**"Detest me after. Live now. *

troisieme_voie.title: The Third Way
troisieme_voie.narrator: Hope is sometimes the last lie we tell each other before choosing.
troisieme_voie.text:
You're asking for another solution.

The doctor speaks fast.

**"Partial sampling will not suffice. *

Nora clings to this sentence.

**"But can it help? *

**"Too little. Too late. Maybe a few dozen. Maybe nobody. *

The voice announced:

> ESTIMATED MAIN IMPACT: 02 MINUTES

The world does not give you a third way.

Only a slower way to refuse to choose.

choix_sacrifice.title: The World
choix_sacrifice.narrator: Some choices save lives and destroy the one that looked at you.
choix_sacrifice.text:
You sit in the chair.

Nora almost stops breathing.

**"No. *

You want to tell him something perfect.

But there's nothing.

So you just say:

**"I'm sorry. *

She's shaking her head.

Tears fall silent.

*"Me too. *

The doctor launches the protocol.

Behind the window, people don't scream anymore.

The first sample is filled with an almost luminous red.

Nora puts her hand against the window.

You put yours in front.

The light outside becomes white.

choices.text: Give up and leave with Nora

choix_nora.title: Nora
choix_nora.narrator: Saving a person can sometimes mean abandoning the world at its silence.
choix_nora.text:
You choose Nora.

Or maybe she chooses for you.

Maybe there's no difference.

You reach the tunnel exit.

The city is behind you.

The sirens have become far away.

The sky opens in a white flash above Saint-Oriel.

Nora puts you against her.

Not to protect you.

She knows she can't.

Just so you're not alone.

The breath comes later than expected.

Late enough to tell you:

**"I know you may never forgive me. But you're alive. *

fin_partielle.title: Some Lives
fin_partielle.narrator: Sometimes compromises have the taste of blood and insufficient.
fin_partielle.text:
You're refusing full protocol.

But you accept partial sampling.

The doctor knows that's not enough.

Nora knows it's too much.

The machine is taking your blood.

Not everything.

That's enough for you to be wavered.

Not enough to save the world.

Later, we'll say the partial serum saved twenty-seven people.

Or thirty.

Or twelve.

The numbers will change according to those who tell.

Nora stays close to you.

You survive.

So does she.

But between you, there will always be this question:

How many lives are worth half a courage?

fin_sacrifice.title: End — Serum
fin_sacrifice.narrator: The world does not always remember correctly those who save it.
fin_sacrifice.text:
The first doses come out before dawn.

Then others.

Protocol S-12 doesn't save everyone.

No honest story ends like this.

But he saves enough lives for children to breathe in shelters.
Enough for doctors to continue.
Enough for someone to talk about the future.

Nora survives.

She refuses the ceremonies.

She only keeps a picture of you two at the coffee shop.

On the back, she writes:

> You would have hated being called a miracle.

Years later, when the first flowers grow on the roofs of Saint-Oriel, some say the world was saved by a serum.

Nora knows that the world was saved by someone who didn't have enough time to say goodbye.

fin_nora.title: End — Two survivors
fin_nora.narrator: There are worlds we don't save, only people we refuse to lose.
fin_nora.text:
You survive.

Not easily.

Not clean.

The world does not end in one light.

He continues, wounded.

Cities fall.
Shelters are extinguishing.
Radio messages call for help for weeks.

Nora hardly sleeps.

Neither do you.

Sometimes she looks at you like she's still checking her crime breathing.

You want to hate her.

Some days you can do it.

Others, you remember his hand in yours.

One night, far from Saint-Oriel, she asks you:

**"If it were to be done again... would you let me save you again? *

You don't have a big enough answer.

Only silence.

And she still stays.

fin_trop_tard.title: End — Do Not Choose
fin_trop_tard.narrator: Sometimes not choosing is still a choice. But it's not always the most human.
fin_trop_tard.text:
You stay.

Just a second.

Then another one.

There was probably a good reason.

Understand better.
Say the right words.
Don't give up on someone.
Don't make a mistake.

But this time, your hesitation doesn't save anyone.

Not the child.
Not Nora.
Not the people in the shelter.
Not you.

The light comes without noise.

For a moment, everything becomes white.

Then there are no sirens.

No more questions.

More choices.

Only the time you thought you had.

retard_enfant.title: Lost Time
retard_enfant.narrator: Saving someone sometimes takes exactly the time we didn't have.
retard_enfant.text:
You run to the old hospital.

But the street isn't the same.

The military closed the passage.

The crowd crashes against the barriers.

Nora's looking for another road.

His look jumps from one alley to the next.

She's calculating.

She panics.

**"No... No, no, no..."**

In the distance, the metal door of the galleries closes.

The sound is tiny.

Almost nothing.

But you understand right now.

You're too late.

The mother you helped disappears in the crowd with her child living in her arms.

You should feel relief.

You feel it.

But not just.

Nora grabs your hand.

**"Perhaps there is still access through the cellars. *

She's lying.

You know that.

She knows that too.

But she still refuses to let go.

choices.text: Still look for a way out with Nora

fin_humaine.title: End — A Life Saved
fin_humaine.narrator: There are defeats that look strangely like light.
fin_humaine.text:
You can't find another entrance.

Nora hits a locked door until she opens her hands.

**"Open up! *

Nobody answers.

The city is shaking.

Sirens twist in the air.

You think about the child.

His arms around his mother's neck.

To that tiny life that goes on somewhere because you lost a few minutes.

Nora's collapsing against you.

**"See? "** she whispers. **"You see why I wanted to choose for you? *

You're not answering.

There's nothing to answer.

The light comes to the end of the street.

White.

Immense.

Nora shake your hand.

This time, she doesn't shoot you.

She's staying.

And in the last seconds, you understand something horrible and sweet:

You didn't save the world.

You didn't survive.

But somewhere, a child still breathes.

fin_humaine_douce.title: End — No longer running
fin_humaine_douce.narrator: Sometimes the last freedom is not to flee anymore.
fin_humaine_douce.text:
You take Nora's hand.

**"Stop. *

She's shaking her head.

**"No. There must be another road. There's always another road. *

**"Nora. *

Your calm is more likely than a cry.

She understands.

The door is closed.

The shelter is too far away.

The world won't wait.

She's laying her forehead against your shoulder.

**"I tried. *

**"I know. *

Around you, the city seems almost silent.

Far away, a mother runs with her child in her arms.

You don't know if they'll get to a shelter.

But they're running.

They have a chance.

Nora follows your gaze.

She's crying.

Not just because she loses you.

Because she finally understands why you would have chosen the world.

The light is coming.

You close your eyes.

His hand stays in yours.

aider_cafe_suite.title: Those Who Remain
aider_cafe_suite.narrator: When the world collapses, some gestures become bigger than those that do.
aider_cafe_suite.text:
You stay.

The waitress is still shaking.

The old man at the bookstore doesn't understand how to get to the shelter.

A child is looking for his mother between the overturned tables.

Nora's looking at you.

**"We have to go. *

You know she's right.

But you see the hands shaking.

Empty faces.

People who didn't understand fast enough.

Then you help.

You give directions.
You're moving a cabinet that fell in front of the exit.
You're carrying the bookseller to the sidewalk.

Nora ends up helping too.

Not because she wants to.

Because she loves you enough to hate what you're doing.

When you finally get out, the street has changed.

The way to the hospital is off.

choices.text: Trying to reach the shelter again
Help the last ones get down to a cellar
Staying without knowing what to do

fin_cafe.title: End — The Café of the Last Seconds
fin_cafe.narrator: Not all shelters are deep enough to save a body. Some save only what is left of humans.
fin_cafe.text:
The shelter is too far away.

Nora knows before you.

She's watching the street.

Then the coffee.

Then you.

**"You gave our time. *

It's not a reproach.

Not completely.

Inside, the waitress is still breathing.

The bookstore is holding his old radio station against him.

The child found his mother.

You didn't save the city.

You didn't get to the shelter.

But around you, several people are alive a few more minutes because you couldn't leave.

The radio skewers.

An ancient song passes between two emergency messages.

Nora sits next to you behind the overturned counter.

**"I wanted to be selfish enough to save you. *

You say:

**"You were. *

She smiles through her tears.

Outside, the light goes up.

fin_cave.title: End — The Cave
fin_cave.narrator: We don't always choose to survive. Sometimes you only choose where to wait for the end.
fin_cave.text:
You're helping the last ones get down to the coffee cellar.

It's not a real shelter.

Just thick walls.
Dusty bottles.
A flashing light bulb.

Nora closes the door behind you.

She doesn't say there's a chance left.

You appreciate her stopping lying.

The waitress holds the child against her.

The bookseller opens his poetry book.

His voice trembles, but he still reads.

Nobody really understands worms.

It's okay.

For a few seconds, there's a room full of people listening together.

Nora takes your hand in the dark.

**"That's why I wanted to keep you alive. *

**"For what? *

**"For the world to keep someone like you. *

The cellar is shaking.

The light is extinguishing.

triage_retard.title: The Last Civil
triage_retard.narrator: Because you want no one to stay outside, you can end up on the wrong side.
triage_retard.text:
You keep going.

One more person.

Then another one.

Nora's calling you.

The doctor, too.

But an injured teenager can't walk.

You're carrying it to the door.

She's passing.

The door is starting to close.

Nora yells at your name.

You run.

Too late.

The armored door seals between you.

On the other side, Nora hits.

Again.

Again.

The doctor says something you can't hear.

Protocol S-12 will not be launched.

You're on the wrong side.

But the teenager's in it.

And several others with her.

choices.text: Put your hand on the door

fin_derriere_porte.title: End — Behind the Door
fin_derriere_porte.narrator: A closed door can save lives on both sides, but not in the same way.
fin_derriere_porte.text:
You put your hand on the armored door.

On the other side, Nora probably poses hers.

You can't see her.

You can't hear it.

But you know her.

You can imagine her rage.
His fear.
His way of refusing reality again.

The automatic voice announces full sealing.

Then silence.

Protocol S-12 will not save the world.

But the civilians you brought in may live.

The teenage girl.
The waitress.
Kids.
The old man.

A few lives.

Not enough for history books.

Enough for your choice not to be empty.

When the light comes, you keep your hand against the door.

So Nora knows, on the other side, you were still there.

tramway_matin.title: The Morning Tramway
tramway_matin.narrator: Normal days are full of details that we believe to be eternal.
tramway_matin.text:
The tram squeaks in the corners.

A woman sleeps against the window.
A student revises without really reading.
Someone listens to music too loud.

Your phone's still vibrating.

Nora:

> If you're still late I also eat your coffee.

An old ad goes on the car screens.

> VISIT THE HISTORIC ABBRISES OF HISTORIAL

Nobody looks up.

The driver announces an unusual slowdown near the military quarter.

Then the routine resumes.

As if the world still hesitated.

souvenir_nora.title: The Blue Bench
souvenir_nora.narrator: We stick to people a long time before we understand that we love them.
souvenir_nora.text:
When you come down the tram, you pass an old blue bench.

Nora always calls it "the depressive bench" because it looks directly at a parking lot.

You're thinking about a winter night.

She waited for you here for two hours after your car accident.

When you asked her why she stayed in the cold, she said:

**"Because someone had to be there when you woke up. *

You laughed.

She doesn't.

Still today, you don't really know if that sentence was a joke.

choices.text: Send Nora a photo of the bench

fete_foraine.title: The Closed Feast
fete_foraine.narrator: Abandoned places sometimes resemble memories that refuse to die.
fete_foraine.text:
The old carnival has been closed for years.

Yet Nora always insists on going ahead.

The big wheel grins with the wind.

Dead lights still hang under the stands.

Nora points to a rusty shooter.

**"Remember when you tried to win this horrible plush? *

You remember most of all that she kept the stuffed stuff.

She notices your look.

**"She's still at my house. *

Then she adds, more slowly:

**"I keep a lot of things useless. *

choices.text: Ask her why she kept it.

confidence_legere.title: Needless Things
confidence_legere.narrator: People rarely talk directly about love. They're talking about things they never threw away.
confidence_legere.text:
Nora lifts her shoulders.

**"I don't know. I like to keep things that remind me that some days have existed. *

She's watching the river.

The wind lifts his hair.

For a second, she seems tired.

Not physically.

Like someone who already knows something is going to be taken over.

Then she finds her smile.

**"Good. If the world ends today, at least the plush may survive. *

You're laughing.

In the distance, a very discreet siren sounds.

Too far to worry anyone.

choices.text: Ask her why she says that

station_metro.title: The Unloaded Station
station_metro.narrator: The panicked crowds look like waves that would have forgotten where to go.
station_metro.text:
The metro station overflows with people.

Families run down the stairs.

An agent is still trying to give indications in a saturated speaker.

Nora's moving fast.

Too fast.

Like someone who already knows the exact times.

On a dock, a musician continues to play the violin.

Nobody listens to him.

Then the current jumps for a second.

Black swallows everything.

When the lights come back, many people already cry.

metro_aide.title: Under the Neon
metro_aide.narrator: Even at the edge of the end, some still stop to reach out.
metro_aide.text:
You help the old woman get up.

She tightens your sleeve like you're the last stable thing in the world.

Nora's coming back to you despite the crowd.

She seems angry.

Then she sees the woman shaking.

Then she helps him too.

That contradiction again.

That way she wants to save everyone except when it comes to you.

In the distance, a rumbling makes the rails vibrate.

Not a train.

Something much further.

And much bigger.

message_banc.title: The Message
message_banc.narrator: Some conversations already look like memories before they are finished.
message_banc.text:
You're sending the picture from the bench.

Three small points appear almost immediately.

Nora:

> Oh, no.
> The depressive bench.
> Are you trying to make me nostalgic in the morning?

You keep walking.

Nora sends a second photo.

The old stuffed carnival.

Always sitting on a shelf at home.

> I told you I kept things useless.

Then:

> Hurry up anyway.
> Coffee cools.

For a few seconds, the world still looks exactly like it was yesterday.

marche_couverte.title: The Covered Market
marche_couverte.narrator: The most ordinary places become sacred once gone.
marche_couverte.text:
By crossing the covered market, Nora stops in front of a fruit seller.

She hesitates long before buying a fishing.

**"It's ridiculous how much it costs now. *

Then she cuts it in half with the little knife in her pocket.

She's giving you half.

The taste is incredibly normal.

Around you, people talk about weather, work, football.

A suspended screen broadcasts political debates without sound.

Nora looks at the pictures.

Then turn away the eyes immediately.

Like someone trying to forget something she already knows.

choices.text: Ask her if anything bothers her.

pharmacie.title: Pharmacy
pharmacie.narrator: People become themselves very quickly when they think they lack time.
pharmacie.text:
A pharmacy is being stormed.

People are screaming.

Some are trying to take medication.
Others are simply looking for water.

A woman begs for insulin.

Nora slows down.

You immediately see the conflict in her eyes.

Leave.
Or help again.

The pharmacist eventually threw boxes over the counter to calm the crowd.

Someone's crying behind you.

Someone's laughing nervously.

The world is already beginning to crack into incompatible pieces.

choices.text: Help the woman get her medication back

famille_nora.title: Nora's Family
famille_nora.narrator: People rarely talk about their family when they're afraid to lose it.
famille_nora.text:
Nora is silent for a few seconds.

Then she sighs.

"My mother still thinks I'm gonna finish up history teacher. *

She smiles weakly.

**"She hates my current job. She says government labs smell secrets and migraines. *

You're laughing.

Nora looks at the rain behind the window.

*"My father collected old radios. When I was little, he sometimes woke me up at night just to listen to weird frequencies coming from across the world. *

She's looking down.

**"I think that's when I started to be afraid of sirens. *

For a second, she seems about to add something.

Then she's happy.

radio_enfance.title: Nora's Father's Radios
radio_enfance.narrator: Childhood memories sometimes become warnings that are understood too late.
radio_enfance.text:
Nora turns her spoon slowly.

"My father always said people think the end of the world would make a huge noise. *

She smiled sadly.

**"But according to him, the worst time would be right before. When everything goes on normally while someone, somewhere, already knows. *

You ask her if she often thinks about that.

She hesitates.

**"For a few weeks? Yes. *

Silence falls.

In the coffee shop, someone drops coins.

A couple laughs by the window.

The coffee machine is rumbling.

The world continues.

A little more.

nora_fissure_avant.title: Almost an Aveu
nora_fissure_avant.narrator: Some secrets start with an interrupted sentence.
nora_fissure_avant.text:
Nora opens her mouth.

**"If something really happens..."**

It stops.

You wait.

Her phone is vibrating.

She's looking at him.

His face is fading for a fraction of a second.

Then it immediately locks the screen.

**"Nothing. Forget it. *

But now you notice that she looks at the hour every thirty seconds.

Like someone waiting for an invisible impact.

disquaire.title: The Discquarian
disquaire.narrator: Songs often stay longer than cities.
disquaire.text:
Nora stops in front of an old store.

The shop broadcasts an ancient song on the street.

She smiles immediately.

*"My brother loved this group. *

You never heard of her brother.

She notices your expression.

**"He left to live in the north years ago. We're almost never called. *

Then she adds:

**"We still think we'll have time later. *

The seller changes his record.

The song is brutally cut.

The silence suddenly seems strange.

regrets_nora.title: Les Choses Discounts à Plus Tard
regrets_nora.narrator: Regret often resembles a conversation we thought we had tomorrow.
regrets_nora.text:
Nora keeps eyes on the window.

**"Yes. *

No joke this time.

**"I spent so much time believing that people would stay there. My family. My friends. You."**

She laughs nervously.

**"That's stupid, isn't it? As if the world owed us time. *

An ambulance passes away.

Too fast.

Too early in the day.

Nora follows her from the look until she disappears.

Then she whispers:

**"If something happened today, I think I'd especially regret everything I've never said. *

choices.text: Ask her what she never said.
Tell him you still have time.

presque_amour.title: Some Important Thing
presque_amour.narrator: Important words rarely reach the end.
presque_amour.text:
Nora's looking at you.

Really.

Like she was trying to memorize your face.

**"I think I should have told you..."**

A siren sounds in the distance.

Not the alarm yet.

Just an ambulance.

But that's enough to stop it.

She turns her eyes away.

**"Nothing. *

Then she starts walking faster.

Like someone fleeing a sentence.
